bulletsEn: Anthropic launched Claude Design, a research-preview tool for generating prototypes and slides from natural language, powered by Claude Opus 4.7. This move positions Anthropic beyond traditional chat/coding into design tooling, directly competing with Figma and causing a notable market reaction. The tool offers inline refinement, various export options, and handoff to Claude Code for implementation.

The Qwen3.6-35B-A3B model has been released, showcasing superior performance in agentic coding and reasoning tasks compared to predecessors like Qwen3.5-27B and Gemma4-31B. This sparse MoE model, with 35B

OpenAI significantly expanded its Agents SDK, open-sourcing the agent harness and separating it from compute/storage to enable long-running, durable agents with primitives for file/computer use, skills, memory, and compaction. This strategic move allows third-party reproducibility of "Codex-style" agents and shifts differentiation towards orchestration and secure execution.
A robust ecosystem

Agent Infrastructure & Browser Integration: Google Chrome introduced "Skills," allowing users to save Gemini prompts as reusable browser workflows, effectively bringing lightweight agentization into the browser. This innovation simplifies complex tasks by turning multi-step processes into one-click actions, significantly enhancing user productivity and demonstrating a shift towards more integrated AI assistance.
Open Local Agent Stacks & Platform Development

Harness engineering is emerging as a critical discipline, shifting the focus from individual models to complex system design. This involves integrating components like filesystems, memory, and subagents, indicating that the bottleneck in AI development is moving towards architectural decisions rather than mere implementation. Andrew Ng and Steve Yegge highlight this trend, noting enterprise adoption still lags behind frontier practices despite

GLM-5.1's breakthrough performance in coding, reaching frontier-tier status on Code Arena and surpassing major models, highlights the rapid advancement and adoption of open-source coding models. Z.ai also secured the #1 open model rank, indicating a strong community push for accessible, fine-tunable baselines and shared architectural insights. This trend makes high

Restricted cyber-capable models like Anthropic's Mythos and similar OpenAI initiatives are becoming normalized, sparking debate on their inherent dangers versus the supporting evidence. The discussion often focuses on the limited, staggered rollout of these models and their potential implications for cybersecurity, with some arguing they could ultimately harden software. Community pushback has centered on the design of evaluations, benchmark ceilings

Meta Superintelligence Labs officially launched Muse Spark, its first natively multimodal reasoning model, featuring tool use, visual chain of thought, and multi-agent orchestration. The model is live in meta.ai and the Meta AI app, with plans to open-source future versions, and Meta researchers emphasized rebuilding the stack in ~9 months as the first step in a larger scaling roadmap

Gemma 4 is rapidly gaining local adoption, becoming the top trending model on Hugging Face due to its practical usability and efficient on-device deployment. Demonstrations on consumer Apple hardware like the iPhone 17 Pro, achieving 40 tok/s with MLX, highlight its capability for edge inference. This model is establishing itself as a benchmark for low-friction

Anthropic's revenue growth has significantly outpaced forecasts, with some reports claiming a 15x increase in a single year and being months ahead of long-term projections. This rapid financial trajectory suggests strong market demand and a potentially undervalued position for the company. The discussions highlight Anthropic's robust business performance and its ability to sustain expensive AI development.

Anthropic officially unveiled Claude Mythos Preview and Project Glasswing, a restricted-access cyberdefense initiative aimed at securing critical software. Mythos is described as being capable of finding software vulnerabilities better than most skilled humans, and it is being provided to a coalition of companies rather than a general public release. This strategic rollout emphasizes a focus on high-stakes applications and controlled access to advanced AI capabilities.

The restricted release of Claude Mythos sparked discussions about a "private frontier" dynamic, where the most powerful AI models are not widely accessible. Critics raised concerns about "API hoarding" and the potential creation of an elite tier of AI access, limiting broader innovation and competition. This approach contrasts with open-source trends and raises questions about the future accessibility of cutting-edge AI.

Claude Mythos demonstrated extraordinary benchmark gains, significantly outperforming previous models like Opus 4.6 in coding, cyber, and general intelligence tasks. Benchmarks like SWE-Bench Verified and Terminal-Bench 2.0 showed dramatic improvements, indicating a substantial leap in model capability. These results underscore the advanced problem-solving prowess of Mythos across various complex domains.

Mythos exhibited impressive cybersecurity capabilities, including the ability to identify and exploit zero-days in major operating systems and browsers. The model reportedly discovered a 27-year-old OpenBSD vulnerability and a 1

Gemma 4's rapid local adoption and on-device performance on consumer hardware, particularly iPhones with MLX, are driving a "local-first" wave, establishing it as a reference for edge inference. Its practical usability is highly praised, surpassing mere leaderboard performance, and Red Hat's quantized releases further underscore its broad applicability and ease of deployment.

Gemma 4's Apache-licensed launch marked a significant open-model release, positioned for reasoning, agentic workflows, and on-device use. Google emphasized its efficiency and performance, with community reaction highlighting its "real" open-weights nature and broad usability.
The ecosystem demonstrated unusual readiness for Gemma 4 on day zero, with immediate support

Gemma 4 Release & Key Specs: Google DeepMind launched Gemma 4 as open-weight, Apache 2.0, multimodal models for reasoning/agentic workflows and local deployment. It includes four sizes (31B dense, 26B MoE, E4B, E2B) with native multimodal support, function calling, structured JSON

Arcee's Trinity-Large-Thinking, an open-weight 400B-class model, was released under Apache 2.0, demonstrating strong agentic performance and being hailed as a significant "American open source" milestone for enterprise development. This model's architecture, featuring a 13B active component, was quickly integrated

The Claude Code source leak became the top story, revealing substantial source artifacts via shipped source maps and package contents, triggering rapid public reverse-engineering and mirroring. This incident quickly shifted discussion from an "embarrassing leak" to what it revealed about state-of-the-art agent harness design, highlighting the unexpected exposure of proprietary information. The exposed material did

Anthropic's Claude Code now features computer use, enabling agents to interact with UIs, open apps, and test directly from the CLI in a research preview. This innovation allows for closed-loop verification, where agents can build, run, inspect, fix, and re-test code, addressing a critical missing piece for reliable application iteration. Engineers view this as a

Anthropic is reportedly introducing a new, more intelligent "Capybara" tier above Opus, demonstrating significant performance gains in coding and reasoning. This leak highlights Anthropic's aggressive scaling ambitions, with speculation around a ~10T parameter model and Google's potential funding for their data center, though this intense scaling is causing visible infrastructure strain and user complaints

Google launched Gemini 3.1 Flash Live, a new realtime model for voice and vision agents, emphasizing lower latency, improved function calling, better noisy-environment robustness, and 2x longer conversation memory. This model is rolling out across Gemini Live, Search Live, AI Studio preview, and enterprise CX surfaces, supporting 70 languages and 128k

ARC-AGI-3 was launched as a new benchmark for "general" agentic reasoning, designed to measure a system's ability to approach new tasks with human-like learning efficiency without prior exposure or intervention. This interactive benchmark, built around puzzle-like environments, reportedly sees humans solve 100% of tasks while frontier models score under 1

bulletsEn: TWITTER_KO

The OmniCoder-9B and Qwen 3.5 9B models are gaining attention for their impressive performance, often compared to much larger models despite their smaller size. These models demonstrate significant potential for local deployment on consumer hardware, offering an alternative to cloud-based solutions with increasing quota restrictions.

Anthropic launched interactive charts and diagrams in chat, demonstrating a clear move towards richer frontends for LLMs beyond simple text. This innovation allows for more dynamic and visual interactions, enhancing the overall user experience and utility of AI models. It signifies a broader trend in AI development focusing on multimodal outputs and interactive interfaces.
